display.purpose: One verdict-level line per screen — masthead wordmark, weight-goal ETA statement. Never body copy.

headline.purpose: Page section titles: Roster, This Week, Season.

body.purpose: All reading copy, table cells, descriptions. Max 68ch measure.

label.purpose: Eyebrows, table headers, status badge text. Always uppercase, always tracked.

shadows.name: soft
shadows.purpose: Subtle lift on raised cards at rest.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Dusty-blue filled button for the one primary action on a screen (create team, generate invite link).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(0.5 0.10 250); color: oklch(0.98 0.008 250); border: none; border-radius: 10px; padding: 10px 16px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.44 0.105 250); } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: oklch(0.38 0.10 250); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.5 0.10 250); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: oklch(0.4 0.01 250); border: none; border-radius: 10px; padding: 10px 16px; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(0.964 0.006 250); color: oklch(0.22 0.01 250); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.5 0.10 250); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Label-plus-color pill used on every roster row and athlete header. Swap the two CSS custom properties to retint for attention/off-track/not-logging.
components.html: On track
components.css: .ds-status-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 5px; color: var(--badge-fg); background: var(--badge-bg); font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 999px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Default form field: team name, invite-link label, athlete search.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; background: transparent; border: 1px solid oklch(0.9 0.012 250); border-radius: 10px; padding: 9px 12px; font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(0.22 0.01 250); outline: none; transition: border-color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), box-shadow 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.58 0.012 250); } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: oklch(0.5 0.10 250); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.5 0.10 250 / 0.16); }

components.name: Macro Ring
components.description: Concentric calorie/protein/carb/fat ring for the athlete detail view, adapted from the UPlate athlete app's Apple-Fitness-style rings so the coach's view matches the athlete's own.
components.html: <svg class="ds-macro-ring" viewBox="0 0 120 120" width="120" height="120"><circle class="ds-macro-ring__track" cx="60" cy="60" r="52" /><circle class="ds-macro-ring__value ds-macro-ring__value--calories" cx="60" cy="60" r="52" stroke-dasharray="245 327" /></svg>
components.css: .ds-macro-ring circle { fill: none; stroke-width: 10; stroke-linecap: round; transform: rotate(-90deg); transform-origin: 60px 60px; } .ds-macro-ring__track { stroke: oklch(0.5 0.10 250 / 0.14); } .ds-macro-ring__value--calories { stroke: oklch(0.5 0.10 250); transition: stroke-dasharray 280ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); }

components.name: Roster Row
components.description: One athlete row in the roster table: status badge, name, today's adherence, weight-trend sparkline. Sorted by status severity, not alphabetically.
components.html: Needs attentionJordan LeeHasn't logged in 2 days
components.css: .ds-roster-row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: auto minmax(0,1fr) auto; align-items: center; gap: 16px; padding: 12px 16px; border-top: 1px solid oklch(0.9 0.012 250); cursor: pointer; transition: background 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-roster-row:hover { background: oklch(0.964 0.006 250); } .ds-roster-row__name { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; color: oklch(0.22 0.01 250); } .ds-roster-row__meta { font-size: 0.78125rem; color: oklch(0.58 0.012 250); }

narrative.overview: Not a fitness-tracking app, not a business analytics console — this reads like the clipboard a good coach already carries: quick to scan, unglamorous, built to answer "who needs me today" in one look. The visual system inherits its foundation directly from the sibling UPlate Restaurant Dashboard (same cool-neutral surfaces, the same dusty UPlate blue, the same Fraunces + Inter type pairing, the same restrained spacing and motion), so the two products read as members of the same family. What's new here is layered on top: a small, deliberate vocabulary of status colors (on-track, needs attention, off-track, not logging) built for fast roster triage, and a set of macro/weight components adapted from what already works in the UPlate athlete app's Apple-Fitness-style rings. This system explicitly rejects the generic SaaS dashboard, the overstimulated fintech look, and — specific to this domain — the diet-culture fitness-app aesthetic: no shame streaks, no aggressive red "you failed" banners, no gamified guilt. A flag on this dashboard means "look here," never "this athlete failed."
keyCharacteristics: Cool-neutral light surface, one restrained accent (dusty blue), status color used deliberately and only where it triages athletes.
Fraunces carries the one moment of editorial voice; everything else is quiet Inter.
Status is always label + color, never color alone.
The weight-projection date is always phrased as an estimate, never a promise.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Dusty blue is the only saturated color used for chrome, navigation, and actions. It never competes with the status or macro palettes for attention.

rules.name: The Label-Plus-Color Rule
rules.body: No status, trend, or flag is ever communicated by color alone. Every colored badge carries a text label or icon.

rules.name: The Editorial Restraint Rule
rules.body: Fraunces appears in at most two places per screen: a masthead wordmark and one verdict-level line. Everywhere else is Inter.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadow appears only as a response to state, never as decoration on static content.

dos: Do lead every roster screen with the status badge column.
Do pair every status color with a text label or icon.
Do phrase the weight-goal ETA as an estimate, never a guarantee.
Do keep Fraunces to the masthead wordmark and one verdict line per screen.
Do use plain language for goal status over clinical or percentage-only labels.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard: no gradient hero-metric tiles, no identical icon+heading+text card grids, no "Welcome back, Coach!" hero.
Don't reach for crypto/fintech overstimulation: no animated gradients, neon glows, or glassmorphism cards.
Don't use diet-culture or gamified fitness-app patterns: no shame streaks, no "you failed your goal" banners, no guilt-tripping copy.
Don't let any screen read like a surveillance log of an athlete's every bite.
Don't communicate status, trend, or flags through color alone.
Don't use border-left/border-right accent stripes, gradient text, or decorative glassmorphism anywhere in the chrome.
